name: My Inspiration Cake (1953)
recipeIngredient: Up to 1 tablespoon Water (for spreading consistency)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees F (175 degrees C).
2. Grease and flour two 9-inch round cake pans.
3. Sprinkle pecans evenly over the bottom of both greased and floured pans.
4. In a measuring cup, lightly spoon flour and level off with a straight edge.
5. In a large bowl, combine all cake ingredients except egg whites and chocolate. Beat for 1 1/2 minutes at medium speed.
6. Gradually add egg whites to the mixture and beat again for 1 1/2 minutes.
7. Carefully spoon 1/4 of the batter into each nut-lined pan and sprinkle with grated chocolate.
8. Spoon remaining batter over grated chocolate and spread carefully.
9. Bake in preheated oven for 30-40 minutes, or until golden brown and springs back when lightly touched in center.
10. Cool pans for 10 minutes before removing cakes from pans. Allow them to cool completely.
1. In a small saucepan, combine 1/2 cup sugar, unsweetened chocolate, and 1/4 cup water over low heat. Cook until melted and smooth, stirring constantly.
2. Remove from heat and let it cool slightly.
3. In a small bowl, mix shortening and vanilla with the cooled frosting.
4. Gradually add powdered sugar to the frosting until well blended. Reserve 1/3 cup of white frosting for piping around the edges.
5. To remaining frosting, add cooled chocolate, any remaining powdered sugar, and enough water to reach desired spreading consistency.
6. Assemble cake by placing one layer with nut side up on a serving plate. Spread top with about half a cup of chocolate frosting.
7. Place the second layer with nut side up on top of the first layer.
8. Frost the sides and 1/2 inch around the top edge of the cake using the remaining chocolate frosting.
9. If necessary, thin the reserved white frosting with enough water to achieve desired piping consistency. Pipe around the edges of nuts on top of the cake.
